name: Alto del Legionario — circular from Albuñuelas
name_original: Alto del Legionario — circular desde Albuñuelas
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Albuñuelas traverse a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, dense pine and olive forests, and dramatic gorges. Situated at a high elevation, the region offers significant climbs and descents, providing expansive views of the Sierra de Almijara and distant Sierra Nevada peaks. Cyclists can explore routes that wind through river valleys and past natural springs, encountering varied flora and fauna. The area's physical features present a challenging yet rewarding environment for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Albuñuelas The most popular touring cycling route is Béznar Reservoir Circular, an 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alto del Legionario — circular from Albuñuelas, a difficult 30.5 miles (49.0 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain, providing panoramic vistas. Local touring cyclists also love the Mesón los Prados – Cabra Montes Tunnel loop from Morro de los Prados, a 31.4 miles (50.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with significant climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Albuñuelas is defined by mountainous roads, pine and olive groves, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging ascents to routes through scenic rural areas. The routes in Albuñuelas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Albuñuelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Albuñuelas?
A: Albuñuelas offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 80 options available. While many are challenging, there are also a few easier routes for those seeking a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Albuñuelas?
A: You can expect diverse and often challenging terrain. Routes frequently traverse mountainous areas with significant elevation changes, winding through dense pine and ancient olive forests. You'll also encounter dramatic gorges, river valleys, and natural springs, with many paths offering expansive views of the Sierra de Almijara and distant Sierra Nevada peaks.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Albuñuelas?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its challenging climbs, there are 3 easy touring cycling routes available around Albuñuelas. These routes offer a gentler introduction to the area's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some popular circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many touring cyclists enjoy circular routes that showcase the diverse scenery. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040859139/beznar-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béznar Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding hills. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045598856/alto-del-legionario-circular-from-albunuelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Legionario — circular from Albuñuelas&lt;/a&gt; provides panoramic vistas after a substantial climb.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The routes in Albuñuelas are rich with natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You might encounter impressive gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3823223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Luna Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or discover stunning waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/87627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Verde Waterfalls, Barranco de las Chortales&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4511116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo del Alcázar&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features unique geological formations and offers views towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/126956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Cielo Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Albuñuelas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging yet rewarding climbs, the stunning panoramic views, and the diverse landscapes that include pine forests, olive groves, and river valleys. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also a frequently appreciated aspect.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through historical sites or villages?
A: Yes, some routes offer a glimpse into the region's history and culture. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10302947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albuñuelas Village – Saleres loop from Albuñuelas&lt;/a&gt; allows you to experience local villages. The village of Albuñuelas itself has narrow medieval streets, remains of a castle, and historical sites like the Iglesia del Salvador and the Arabic Tower: Torre del Tio Bayo, which can be explored before or after your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Albuñuelas?
A: Albuñuelas benefits from a favorable climate, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing ample daylight. Winter can also be good, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there any routes that include caves or interesting geological formations?
A: Yes, the region around Albuñuelas is known for its caves. While cycling, you might find yourself near highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Riscos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Funes&lt;/a&gt;. These offer unique points of interest to explore off the bike.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes typically take to complete in Albuñuelas?
A: The duration varies significantly based on the route's distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040859139/beznar-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béznar Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, at 19.3 km, typically takes around 1 hour 28 minutes. More challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31817957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesón los Prados – Cabra Montes Tunnel loop from Morro de los Prados&lt;/a&gt;, which is 50.5 km with significant climbs, can take approximately 3 hours 55 minutes.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on touring cycling routes in Albuñuelas?
A: Touring cycling in Albuñuelas often involves substantial elevation gains due to the mountainous terrain. Many routes feature climbs of 1,000 meters for every 50 km, offering a demanding but rewarding experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31817957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesón los Prados – Cabra Montes Tunnel loop from Morro de los Prados&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 1,380 meters.
tips: After the occasional improvement and retouting, some old segments of the original route can be admired from time to time.
The best time to pass through this area is in spring, all the meadows shine with an intense green color and the place becomes a magical place.
Just a magnificent place to take a break and enjoy the beauty of nature.
